_meta.source: PLACEHOLDER — compiled from GigaGPU, Mustafa.net, NVIDIA NIM benchmarks cited in findings.md §5. Verify against current vendor benchmarks before quoting.
_meta.notes: Throughput figures are Q4_K_M quantized-class estimates for an 8B-parameter model on each GPU at typical consumer/cloud serving settings. Larger models run slower — see data/local_model_profiles.json for per-model overrides. Power costs (electricity, amortization) are operator-supplied at request time and are NOT stored here.

nvidia-rtx-4090.notes: PLACEHOLDER — GigaGPU LLaMA 3 8B @ ~135 tok/s, peak ~245 tok/s

nvidia-rtx-3090.notes: PLACEHOLDER — Mustafa.net LLaMA 3 8B @ 95 tok/s, GigaGPU 85 tok/s

nvidia-a100-80gb.notes: PLACEHOLDER — Lambda Labs benchmarks ~180 tok/s for 8B; scales linearly for batch

nvidia-h100-80gb.notes: PLACEHOLDER — NVIDIA NIM 51 tok/s for Llama-3.3-70B at 1 user; ~350 tok/s for 8B

amd-mi300x.notes: PLACEHOLDER — 192GB HBM3 keeps 70B-class models fully resident

apple-m2-ultra.notes: PLACEHOLDER — unified memory allows large models; slower per-token than discrete GPUs

apple-m3-max.notes: PLACEHOLDER — Mustafa.net LLaMA 3 8B @ ~40 tok/s via MLX

apple-m4-max.notes: PLACEHOLDER — newer generation, faster than M3 Max

cpu-only-xeon-32core.notes: PLACEHOLDER — fallback when no GPU available; expect 5-10 tok/s for quantized 7B
